## What is LiveChat?

LiveChat is a customer communication platform that places a configurable chat widget on your website and routes conversations to agents or AI. It is part of the broader Text platform ecosystem and supports multi-channel communication including Facebook Messenger, Apple Messages for Business, SMS, and WhatsApp alongside its core website chat.

## Who is it for?

LiveChat targets businesses from solo operators to enterprise customer service departments. The Starter plan suits small businesses testing live chat; the Team and Business plans fit full-time support and sales teams. Around 35,000 companies use the platform across retail, SaaS, and services industries.

## Strengths

- **AI-powered tools** — reply suggestions, chat summaries, sentiment analysis, and a Copilot assistant are available across plans.
- **Multi-channel reach** — handles conversations from website chat, Facebook Messenger, SMS, and Apple Messages from one inbox.
- **Visitor tracking** — monitors active website visitors and enables proactive chat triggers based on behavior.
- **Transparent pricing** — clear per-agent pricing with no hidden fees and a 14-day free trial.
- **Proactive engagement** — automated campaigns can trigger chat invitations to visitors based on page or time rules.

## What to consider

- Per-agent billing scales steeply for large teams; costs add up quickly at the Team or Business tier.
- The Starter plan is very limited — one user only, with a 100-visitor tracking cap.
- ChatBot is a separate paid add-on starting at $52/month rather than included in base plans.

## Bottom line

LiveChat is a polished, well-established option for businesses that want to engage website visitors in real time. The AI features and multi-channel support punch above the price point — just watch the per-seat cost if your team is large, and budget for the ChatBot add-on if automation is a priority.
